learning.concept: A program is a specific set of instructions that tells the computer what to do.
learning.explanation:
Computers are fast but dumb. Without a program, they are just expensive paperweights. A program must be:
1. Precise (Not vague)
2. Sequential (One by one)
3. Correct (No mistakes)

analogies.title: The Cooking Recipe
analogies.body:
If you follow a recipe: 
1. Boil Water
2. Add Tea
3. Serve
You get tea. If you swap 1 and 3, you get an empty cup and a wet table! Order matters in a program.

analogies.title: Sheet Music for a Piano
analogies.body: The Piano is the hardware. The Sheet Music is the Program. Without the music, the piano does nothing. The music tells which key to press and how long.

analogies.title: A Construction Plan
analogies.body: Building a house requires a step-by-step plan. Layer bricks first, then roof. If you try to roof before bricks, the plan fails. Computers are the same.

examples.title: Personal App
examples.description: A weather app that reads your location (Input), talks to a weather station (Process), and shows the temperature (Output).

examples.title: Web Browser
examples.description: Chrome or Safari are massive programs that take a web address and transform it into a beautiful webpage.

examples.title: Game Logic
examples.description: A game program defining that falling into lava means taking -20 health and playing a sound effect.

practice.q: What do we call a mistake in a program?
Does a program run all steps at once or one by one?
Who writes the program, the human or the machine?
